growth_records.strain: biofilm community, steady-state period 6 (Snertinge biogas plant digestate inoculum)
growth_records.medium: BA (basal anaerobic) medium with H2/CH4/CO2 gas feed

up.method: community-level uptake bound derived from measured input gas flow rate QIN, converted with ideal-gas molar volume 24.46 L/mol at 298 K and estimated dry mass per L; CO2:H2 fixed at 1:4
community-level uptake bound derived from measured input gas flow rate QIN (same conversion); CO2:H2 fixed at 1:4

sec.method: minimum export bound derived from measured gas flow / reactor CH4 production rate

growth_records.cite: De Bernardini et al. Integrating metagenomic binning with flux balance analysis to unravel syntrophies in anaerobic CO2 methanation. Microbiome (2022). DOI:10.1186/s40168-022-01311-1. [PMC9347119]
growth_records.snippet: The uptake rate (i.e., lower and upper bound) of CO2 and H2 were fixed at a 1:4 ratio, specifically at 1.20 and 5.11 mmol/gDW/h, while the minimum export rate of CH4 was fixed at 1.15 mmol/gDW/h.
growth_records.notes: Community-level (not single-species) flux constraints for a thermophilic (55C) anaerobic H2/CO2-methanating biofilm community. H2 uptake 5.11 and CO2 uptake 1.20 mmol/gDW/h and CH4 export >=1.15 mmol/gDW/h were derived by the authors from the MEASURED input gas flow rate QIN [L/LR.d] converted to mmol/gDW/h using ideal-gas molar volume 24.46 L/mol (298 K) and an estimated biofilm dry mass per liter; CO2:H2 constrained to 1:4 stoichiometry. Input gas H2:CH4:CO2 = 62:23:15. No measured microbial growth rate is reported (member growth rates are FBA-simulated at 0.5x max community growth via cooperative tradeoff), so growth_rate left null. Medium = BA basal anaerobic (composition only in referenced protocol, not numeric in main text) -> medium_composition_stated false. Values are load-bearing GEM boundary conditions and are experimentally grounded via gas-flow measurement.
